back_translation: And this is the commandment, the statutes and the judgments, that the Lord your God commanded to teach you, to do them in the land where you are crossing over to possess it,

so that you may fear the Lord your God, to keep all His statutes and His commandments that I am commanding you—you and your son and your son's son—all the days of your life, and so that your days may be prolonged.

And you shall hear, O Israel, and observe to do, so that it may be well with you, and that you may multiply greatly, as the Lord, the God of your fathers, spoke to you—a land flowing with milk and honey.
Hear, O Israel: the Lord our God, the Lord is one.
And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength.
And these words that I am commanding you today shall be on your heart.
And you shall teach them diligently to your sons, and you shall speak of them when you sit in your house, and when you walk on the road, and when you lie down, and when you rise up.
And you shall bind them as a sign on your hand, and they shall be as frontlets between your eyes.
And you shall write them on the doorposts of your house and on your gates.
And it shall be when the Lord your God brings you to the land that He swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give you—great and good cities that you did not build,
and houses full of every good thing that you did not fill, and cisterns hewn out that you did not hew, vineyards and olive trees that you did not plant—and you shall eat and be satisfied,
watch yourself, lest you forget the Lord who brought you out from the land of Egypt, from the house of slaves.
The Lord your God you shall fear, and Him you shall serve, and in His name you shall swear.
You shall not walk after other gods, from the gods of the peoples that are around you.
For a jealous God, the Lord your God, is in your midst, lest the anger of the Lord your God be kindled against you and He destroy you from upon the face of the ground.
You shall not test the Lord your God, as you tested Him at Massah.
Keep, you shall keep the commandments of the Lord your God, and His testimonies, and His statutes that He commanded you.
And you shall do the right and the good in the eyes of the Lord, so that it may be well with you, and you may enter and possess the good land that the Lord swore to your fathers,
to drive out all your enemies from before you, as the Lord has spoken.
When your son asks you tomorrow, saying: What are the testimonies and the statutes and the judgments that the Lord our God commanded you?
Then you shall say to your son: We were slaves to Pharaoh in Egypt, and the Lord brought us out from Egypt with a strong hand.
And the Lord gave signs and wonders, great and evil, in Egypt, against Pharaoh and against all his house, before our eyes.
And us He brought out from there, in order to bring us, to give us the land that He swore to our fathers.
And the Lord commanded us to do all these statutes, to fear the Lord our God, for good to us all the days, to keep us alive as it is this day.
And righteousness will be ours when we keep to do all this commandment before the Lord our God, as He commanded us.
